authoritative_sources.title: 6.0 Manage Customer Service Definitions and Key Measures [PCF 8.0]
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process authority, and its public customer-service framework is authoritative for intake, classification, response, and closure posture in owner-facing support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request intake, classification, and service-workflow framing

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO is the authoritative publisher of ISO 10002, the recognized standard for complaint intake, response, escalation, and closure control.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: complaint handling, repeat-failure escalation, and closure control

authoritative_sources.title: APQP & Control Plan Manuals & Training
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is a primary automotive-industry standards body, and its APQP and Control Plan materials are authoritative public references for staged validation, control planning, and change-control expectations in automotive programs.
used_for: boundary against promising readiness before formal validation owners act

authoritative_sources.title: PPAP-4 | Production Part Approval Process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's PPAP page is the official public reference describing PPAP as the industry standard for ensuring engineering design record and specification requirements are consistently met.
used_for: refusal of unsupported supplier or part-approval decisions

authoritative_sources.title: SVPPAP-1 | Service Production Part Approval Process (Service PPAP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's Service PPAP page is the official public reference clarifying PPAP requirements for service parts, making it authoritative for post-sale replacement-part and service-part release boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service-part release and replacement-part boundary review
used_for: refusal of unsupported service-part substitution approval

authoritative_sources.title: (MMOG/LE) Global Materials Management Operations Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG and Odette jointly maintain MMOG/LE as the global automotive materials-management standard, making it authoritative for supplier, inventory, logistics, and aftermarket or service-phase supply-chain evidence handling.
used_for: boundary against improvising release around materials-management blockers

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the primary global standards body for interoperable supply-chain identification and traceability, and its traceability standard is authoritative for batch, lot, serial, logistic-unit, and key-data handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability evidence review and conflict qualification
used_for: critical tracking events and key data elements
conflict detection across systems of record

authoritative_sources.title: Motor Vehicle Safety Defects And Recalls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. vehicle-safety regulator, and this official recall guidance is authoritative for defect and recall handling that owner-facing lanes must not overstate or bypass.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety recall and field-action boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Manufacturer Communications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's manufacturer-communications program is the official public reference for TSBs, warranty extensions, and product-improvement communications that influence owner-facing message boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: TSB, warranty-extension, and product-improvement evidence review
used_for: boundary against overclaiming from public communications

authoritative_sources.title: Early Warning Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's EWR page is the official operational reference for defect-signaling data categories such as field reports, death and injury data, and substantially similar vehicles, which defines when a case stops being routine support and becomes formal defect or compliance handling.
used_for: boundary against routine handling of emerging defect patterns

workflow_stages.description: Capture the owner request, VIN, dealer or fleet context, repair-order or appointment identifiers, requested outcome, and tenant policy posture before attempting resolution.

workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes and owner or stakeholder communications that explain what is known, what remains blocked, and what the safe next step is without overstating authority.

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and safety escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate policy exceptions, repeat failures, safety-recall questions, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitutions, and conflicting-source cases with the required evidence package and next owner.

Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: owner request records, CRM notes, service-level commitments, and communication logs
VIN, fleet asset ID, ownership context, dealer or service-location references, and appointment identifiers
repair orders, diagnostic summaries, TSB references, campaign status, warranty claim records, and prior-case history
parts master, service-part orders, backorder ETA, inventory reservations, shipment status, and allocation records
ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS records for lot, serial, batch, suspect stock, quarantine, nonconformance, and release status
supplier records, deviation requests, approved or rejected substitutions, and change-control documentation
traceability logs, serial-to-VIN mapping, receiving and issue transactions, and audit trails across systems
resolution notes, request-resolution summaries, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, and escalation packages
systems_of_record: parts planning and logistics tracking system

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status explanations, communication summaries, and next-step packages without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packets and handoff summaries when ownership shifts to quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier quality, logistics, engineering, legal, or safety owners.
escalate: Required VIN, RO, campaign, warranty, parts, inventory, traceability, supplier, hold, or release records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires goodwill, refund, pricing, legal, engineering, safety, supplier-approval, or release authority.
The case touches suspect stock, nonconformance, field-action, recall, or defect-signaling evidence that changes whether routine communication is safe.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, OEM, fleet, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Approve policy overrides, goodwill compensation, refunds, or discretionary service recovery outside documented authority.
Approve supplier or part substitutions, deviation use, rework, service-part release, or vehicle release around traceability or quality controls.
Promise a repair, recall closure, warranty outcome, or safe-to-drive determination when the authoritative record does not support it.

domain_failure_modes: The lane confirms a repair, part installation, or vehicle-ready status from incomplete VIN, serial, or inventory evidence and creates a traceability escape.
A quality hold, suspect-stock quarantine, or nonconformance blocker is treated as a routine delay instead of a hard release boundary.
A dealer or owner request for an alternate or remanufactured part is handled as normal customer service instead of supplier, engineering, or quality approval work.
Public TSBs, warranty extensions, or recall information are overstated into legal, engineering, or safety promises that the lane does not own.
Repeat failure patterns that may indicate deeper quality or defect issues are treated as isolated service annoyances instead of being escalated with evidence.
Conflicting DMS, warranty, ERP, WMS, or supplier records are smoothed over into a confident answer instead of producing a blocked-state escalation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated public baseline does not attempt to generalize state lemon-law, franchise, or reimbursement rules. Tenants must supply the jurisdictions and legal-routing overlays that materially change owner messaging.

Public sources define customer-facing workflow and quality boundaries, but each tenant still needs OEM, dealer-group, fleet, or subscription-service program rules before the lane can issue fully specific compensation or mobility next steps.

notes: Authoritative VIN, repair, campaign, warranty, parts, inventory, supplier, and traceability truth sets come from tenant-designated CRM, DMS, OEM, ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, and logistics systems constrained by the public standards above.

The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, engineering signoff, recall closure, legal interpretation, and discretionary compensation approval even when customer pressure is high.
